title: UV-Net: Learning from Boundary Representations
abstract: We introduce UV-Net, a novel neural network architecture and representation designed to operate directly on Boundary representation (B-rep) data from 3D CAD models. UV-Net exploits the U and V parameter domain of curves and surfaces to model geometry, and an adjacency graph to explicitly model topology. We present a synthetic dataset called SolidLetters and demonstrate that UV-Net outperforms point clouds, voxels, and meshes across multiple tasks.
method_summary: UV-Net uses UV-grid sampling of B-rep face surfaces as input features and a face adjacency graph for topology. A 1D CNN processes UV-grids per face, then a GNN aggregates features over the topology graph for classification and segmentation.

title: BRepNet: A Topological Message Passing System for Solid Models
abstract: BRepNet is a neural network designed to work directly with B-rep data structures used in CAD. Rather than converting models to meshes or point clouds, the architecture defines convolutional kernels with respect to oriented coedges in the data structure. The authors release the Fusion 360 Gallery segmentation dataset containing over 35,000 annotated B-rep models.
method_summary: BRepNet defines coedge-centric convolutions that pass messages along the topological structure of B-rep models. It uses walking operators on coedges to aggregate information from adjacent faces, edges, and vertices.

key_results.fusion360_segmentation: higher accuracy than mesh/point cloud methods

title: CAD-MLLM: Unifying Multimodality-Conditioned CAD Generation With MLLM
abstract: CAD-MLLM is a unified system for generating parametric CAD models from diverse inputs including text, images, point clouds, or combinations thereof. It leverages LLMs to align multimodal data with CAD command sequences. The authors introduce Omni-CAD, the first multimodal CAD dataset containing textual descriptions, multi-view images, points, and command sequences for approximately 450K CAD models.
method_summary: Uses frozen encoders for each non-text modality with projection layers to align with LLM token space. The LLM generates CAD command sequences conditioned on multimodal inputs. Introduces Omni-CAD dataset with 450K instances.

title: ContrastCAD: Contrastive Learning-based Representation Learning for Computer-Aided Design Models
abstract: ContrastCAD uses contrastive learning to capture semantic information within CAD construction sequences. It generates augmented views using dropout techniques without altering shape, and proposes Random Replace and Extrude (RRE) augmentation for imbalanced datasets. The approach produces better clustered representation spaces for similar CAD models.
method_summary: Applies contrastive learning to CAD construction sequences with dropout-based augmentation and a novel RRE data augmentation method. Learns representations that are robust to sequence permutations.

key_results.representation_quality: improved clustering of similar CAD models

title: SolidGen: An Autoregressive Model for Direct B-rep Synthesis
abstract: SolidGen is an autoregressive neural network that models B-rep directly by predicting vertices, edges, and faces using Transformer-based and pointer neural networks. It uses an Indexed Boundary Representation to capture geometric and topological relations, and can be conditioned on class labels, images, and voxels.
method_summary: Autoregressive generation of B-rep elements (vertices, edges, faces) using Transformers and pointer networks. Introduces Indexed Boundary Representation for capturing topology.

key_results.brep_generation: direct B-rep synthesis without CAD sequence supervision

title: JoinABLe: Learning Bottom-up Assembly of Parametric CAD Joints
abstract: JoinABLe is a learning-based method that assembles parts together to form joints using weak supervision from standard parametric CAD files. It makes network predictions over a graph representation of solid models, achieving 79.53% accuracy approaching human performance. The authors release the Fusion 360 Gallery assembly dataset with rich joint and contact information.
method_summary: Uses graph neural networks on B-rep face/edge representations of individual parts. Predicts joint type, axis, and parameters through edge convolution between part graphs. Trained with weak supervision from CAD joint definitions.

title: BRepMAE: Self-Supervised Masked BRep Autoencoders for Machining Feature Recognition
abstract: BRepMAE is a masked self-supervised learning framework that automatically extracts representations from CAD models for machining feature recognition. It uses geometric Attributed Adjacency Graphs from B-rep and a masked graph autoencoder focusing on reconstructing geometries and attributes of B-rep facets.
method_summary: Converts B-rep to geometric Attributed Adjacency Graphs, then applies masked autoencoding to reconstruct randomly masked face geometries and attributes. Pre-trained encoder transfers to downstream MFR tasks with limited labeled data.

key_results.few_shot_recognition: strong performance with limited training samples

title: Masked BRep Autoencoder via Hierarchical Graph Transformer
abstract: A self-supervised framework combining masked graph autoencoder with hierarchical graph Transformer using cross-scale attention for learning CAD B-rep representations. Reconstructs masked geometries and attributes for representation learning, then replaces decoder with task-specific networks for classification and feature recognition.
method_summary: Combines masked graph autoencoding with a hierarchical graph Transformer that uses cross-scale attention to capture both local face features and global model structure. Two-stage: self-supervised pre-training then supervised fine-tuning.

title: BrepCoder: A Unified Multimodal Large Language Model for Multi-task B-rep Reasoning
abstract: BrepCoder is a unified MLLM that performs diverse CAD tasks from B-rep inputs by converting CAD modeling sequences into Python-like code and aligning them with B-rep. Two-stage training: first reverse engineering to learn geometric features, then downstream tasks like completion and error correction.
method_summary: Converts B-rep to code-like representation, uses multimodal LLM architecture to align B-rep geometry with language. Two-stage training enables generalization across reverse engineering, completion, and error correction tasks.

key_results.multi_task: superior generalization across diverse CAD tasks

title: BrepLLM: Native Boundary Representation Understanding with Large Language Models
abstract: BrepLLM enables LLMs to parse and reason over raw B-rep data. Uses adaptive UV sampling and hierarchical encoder for cross-modal alignment pre-training, followed by multi-stage fine-tuning with Mixture-of-Query Experts. Constructed dataset of 269,444 Brep-text pairs.
method_summary: Two-stage pipeline: cross-modal alignment pre-training with adaptive UV sampling of B-rep surfaces, then multi-stage fine-tuning with Mixture-of-Query Experts for task-specific reasoning. Bridges structured 3D geometry and natural language.

title: Geometric Deep Learning for Computer-Aided Design: A Survey
abstract: Survey examining how geometric deep learning transforms CAD workflows, covering similarity analysis and retrieval, 2D and 3D CAD model synthesis, CAD generation from point clouds and images, along with benchmark datasets and open-source implementations.
method_summary: Comprehensive survey covering B-rep learning, point cloud methods, sequence-based approaches, and multi-modal methods for CAD. Reviews datasets, benchmarks, and open challenges.

key_results.survey: comprehensive coverage of geometric DL for CAD

title: OpenShape: Scaling Up 3D Shape Representation Towards Open-World Understanding
abstract: OpenShape learns multi-modal joint representations of text, image, and point clouds using contrastive learning. Achieves 46.8% zero-shot accuracy on Objaverse-LVIS and 85.3% on ModelNet40. Supports fine-grained text-3D and image-3D interactions.
method_summary: Multi-modal contrastive learning across text, image, and point cloud modalities. Scales training data through multiple 3D datasets, filters and enriches text descriptions, and implements hard negative mining for better alignment.

title: BRT: Bringing Attention to CAD - Boundary Representation Learning via Transformer
abstract: BRT proposes a Transformer-based approach for processing B-rep models. Addresses irregular topology and continuous geometry through continuous geometric embedding encoding B-rep surfaces into Bezier triangles and topology-aware embeddings. Achieves state-of-the-art in part classification and feature recognition.
method_summary: Encodes B-rep surfaces as Bezier triangles for continuous geometric embedding, adds topology-aware positional embeddings, then processes with standard Transformer architecture for classification and segmentation.

title: QueryCAD: Grounded Question Answering for CAD Models
abstract: QueryCAD enables extracting information from CAD models using natural language questions. Includes SegCAD, an open-vocabulary instance segmentation model for identifying specific parts based on descriptions, plus a benchmark for evaluation.
method_summary: Combines open-vocabulary instance segmentation (SegCAD) with language-based querying to ground natural language questions on specific CAD model parts. Integrates with robot program synthesis.

key_results.grounded_qa: first system for grounded QA on CAD models

title: HG-CAD: Material Prediction for Design Automation Using Graph Representation Learning
abstract: HG-CAD introduces a graph representation learning framework for material prediction in CAD assemblies. Formulates material selection as node-level prediction on assembly graphs, jointly learning body-level and assembly-level features.
method_summary: Hierarchical graph neural network that jointly learns body-level geometric features and assembly-level structural features. Predicts material assignments for assembly bodies through node classification on the assembly graph.

title: What's in a Name? Evaluating Assembly-Part Semantic Knowledge in Language Models through User-Provided Names in CAD Files
abstract: Evaluates semantic knowledge of part-part and part-whole relationships in assemblies using language models. Extracts and cleans natural language part, feature, and document names from CAD files, demonstrating that pre-trained LMs outperform numerical methods for assembly semantic tasks.
method_summary: Extracts natural language names from CAD assembly files, evaluates pre-trained language models on tasks like part-assembly matching, part similarity, and hierarchical relationship prediction.

key_results.semantic_knowledge: LMs outperform numerical baselines on assembly semantics

title: TAMM: TriAdapter Multi-Modal Learning for 3D Shape Understanding
abstract: TAMM addresses limited 3D shape datasets by transferring knowledge from 2D images and language. Proposes tri-adapter architecture to better exploit both image and language modalities for 3D shape understanding, addressing the finding that image modality contributes less than language in existing methods.
method_summary: Three adapters bridge pre-trained CLIP image/text encoders with a 3D point cloud encoder. Uses modality-specific and cross-modal adapters to balance contributions from image and language modalities for 3D understanding.

title: CAD-Assistant: Tool-Augmented VLLMs as Generic CAD Task Solvers
abstract: CAD-Assistant is a general-purpose CAD agent using a Vision and Large Language Model as planner with tool-augmentation using CAD-specific Python libraries. Generates and executes FreeCAD code from multimodal inputs including text, images, and existing CAD models.
method_summary: Uses VLLM as a planner that generates FreeCAD Python code, executed within CAD software. Iterative refinement loop with visual feedback. Handles diverse CAD tasks through tool augmentation rather than end-to-end generation.

key_results.generic_cad_tasks: first general-purpose CAD agent with tool augmentation

title: Boundary and Shape Representation Alignment via Self-Supervised Transformers
abstract: Self-supervised method that aligns boundary representations with shape representations using Transformers. Learns to predict dense spatial points from parametric Bezier control points, enabling understanding of physical manifolds from B-rep data.
method_summary: Self-supervised pre-training task where the model predicts dense surface points from parametric Bezier control points of B-rep surfaces. Uses Transformer architecture for cross-modal alignment between boundary and shape representations.

title: CadVLM: Bridging Language and Vision in the Generation of Parametric CAD Sketches
abstract: CadVLM is an end-to-end vision language model for CAD generation. Adapts pre-trained foundation models to manipulate engineering sketches, bridging language and vision for parametric CAD sketch generation.
method_summary: Adapts pre-trained VLM foundation models with CAD-specific fine-tuning. Processes engineering sketch images and text instructions to generate parametric CAD sketch sequences.

key_results.sketch_generation: superior performance in CAD sketch generation tasks

title: Synthesizing CAD Assemblies in Fusion 360
abstract: Addresses repetitive steps in CAD assembly design by developing methods to synthesize assemblies in Fusion 360. Proposes approaches for inserting parts and creating assembly relationships for product lines and design families.
method_summary: Plugin-based approach for Fusion 360 that annotates parts with types and manages subtype hierarchies. Synthesizes assembly programs by learning from existing assembly patterns.

title: Generative 3D Part Assembly via Part-Whole-Hierarchy Message Passing
abstract: Proposes part-whole-hierarchy message passing for 3D part assembly. Leverages that super-part poses provide strong hints about part poses and that predicting super-part poses is easier due to fewer super-parts.
method_summary: Hierarchical graph learning with two levels: part-level and super-part-level. Message passing between levels propagates assembly constraints from coarse super-part predictions to fine-grained part poses.

title: Fusion 360 Gallery: A Dataset and Environment for Programmatic CAD Construction from Human Design Sequences
abstract: Presents the Fusion 360 Gallery dataset of 8,625 human design sequences with sketch and extrude operations, plus a Pythonic API environment for programmatic CAD construction. Enables learning-based approaches using human CAD design sequences.
method_summary: Curates large-scale dataset of human CAD design sequences from Fusion 360 with B-rep segmentation annotations. Provides gym-like environment for training and evaluating CAD program synthesis methods.

key_results.dataset: 8,625 human design sequences with B-rep annotations

title: SldprtNet: A Large-Scale Multimodal Dataset for CAD Generation in Language-Driven 3D Design
abstract: SldprtNet is a large-scale dataset of over 242,000 industrial parts for semantic-driven CAD modeling, geometric deep learning, and multimodal model training. Supports language-driven 3D design with parametric encoding and decoding.
method_summary: Curates 242K industrial parts with multimodal annotations (3D models, text descriptions, parametric sequences). Provides benchmarks for text-to-CAD generation and CAD understanding tasks.

title: Pointer-CAD: Unifying B-Rep and Command Sequences via Pointer-based Edges & Faces Selection
abstract: Unifies B-Rep and command sequence representations for CAD generation via pointer-based entity selection. Enables complex editing operations (fillet, chamfer) that require explicit face/edge selection, which prior command-sequence methods cannot support.
method_summary: Introduces pointer mechanism allowing LLM-based CAD generators to reference specific B-Rep entities (faces, edges) during command sequence generation, bridging the gap between parametric modeling and B-Rep representation.

title: HoLa: B-Rep Generation using a Holistic Latent Representation
abstract: Learns a holistic latent (HoLa) space for B-rep generation, enabling the first diffusion-based B-rep generator supporting diverse conditional inputs (text, point cloud, multi-view images, 2D sketches). Achieves greatly improved validity over state of the art.
method_summary: Reformulates B-rep topology as geometry of intersecting curves, creating a unified holistic latent space. Diffusion-based generator operates in this space for conditional and unconditional B-rep generation.

title: BrepVGAE: Variational Graph Autoencoder with Unified Latent Representation for B-rep
abstract: First variational graph autoencoder framework capable of holistically encoding and decoding B-rep models. Establishes unified formulation for heterogeneous faces and edges, overcoming limitations of conventional graph-based B-rep representations.
method_summary: Variational graph autoencoder with unified latent space for B-rep faces and edges, enabling both reconstruction and generation of valid B-rep models through graph-based encoding/decoding.

title: Few-Shot B-rep Classification with Hybrid Graph Representation
abstract: Addresses few-shot B-rep classification by constructing hybrid graph representations capturing multi-level structural information: local topology graph, global parallel graph, and regional association graph.
method_summary: Three-level hybrid graph representation (local topology, global parallel, regional association) combined with meta-learning for few-shot B-rep model classification with limited labeled data.

title: High-Fidelity B-Rep Latent Representation and Robust Generation
abstract: Builds compact high-fidelity latent representation for B-rep and reformulates validity constraints as differentiable objectives for robust generation. Achieves high-validity B-rep synthesis.
method_summary: Compact latent B-rep representation with differentiable validity constraints enabling robust generation of valid, high-fidelity B-rep models through constrained optimization.

title: BrepARG: AutoRegressive Generation with B-rep Holistic Token Sequence Representation
abstract: First attempt to encode B-rep geometry and topology into a holistic token sequence representation, enabling sequence-based B-rep generation with an autoregressive Transformer. Encodes B-rep into 3 types of tokens: geometry, position, and face index tokens.
method_summary: Holistic tokenization of B-rep into geometry tokens (face/edge UV-grids), position tokens (spatial coordinates), and face index tokens (topology). Autoregressive Transformer generates the unified sequence.

title: BrepGPT: Autoregressive B-rep Generation with Voronoi Half-Patch
abstract: Single-stage autoregressive framework for B-rep generation using Voronoi Half-Patch (VHP) representation. Decomposes B-reps into unified local units by assigning geometry to nearest half-edges and sampling their next pointers. Achieves state-of-the-art unconditional B-rep generation.
method_summary: Voronoi Half-Patch representation decomposes B-reps into unified local units. Single-stage autoregressive generation without cascaded multi-stage networks, reducing error accumulation.

title: AutoBrep: Autoregressive B-Rep Generation with Unified Topology and Geometry
abstract: Transformer model that autoregressively generates B-Reps with high quality and validity. Unified tokenization scheme encoding both geometric and topological characteristics as discrete tokens. Highly scalable to complex solids.
method_summary: Unified tokenization of B-rep geometry (surfaces, curves as Bezier patches) and topology (face-edge adjacency) into a single discrete token sequence. Decoder-only Transformer for autoregressive generation.

title: CAD-GPT: Synthesising CAD Construction Sequence with Spatial Reasoning-Enhanced Multimodal LLMs
abstract: Introduces CAD-GPT, a CAD synthesis method with spatial reasoning-enhanced MLLM that takes single image or text as input. Uses 3D Modeling Assistant Point (3D-MAP) for precise spatial inference in 3D modeling tasks.
method_summary: 3D Modeling Assistant Point (3D-MAP) representation enables precise spatial reasoning in MLLMs for CAD sequence generation. First MLLM specifically trained for CAD construction sequence synthesis.

title: GenCAD: Image-Conditioned Computer-Aided Design Generation with Transformer-Based Contrastive Representation and Diffusion Priors
abstract: Generative model using autoregressive transformers with contrastive learning and latent diffusion to transform image inputs into parametric CAD command sequences, producing editable 3D CAD models.
method_summary: Contrastive learning aligns image and CAD sequence embeddings, then latent diffusion generates CAD tokens conditioned on image features. Autoregressive transformer decodes the final CAD command sequence.

title: VideoCAD: A Large-Scale Video Dataset for Learning UI Interactions and 3D Reasoning from CAD Software
abstract: Large-scale synthetic dataset of over 41K annotated video recordings of CAD operations for learning long-horizon 3D CAD UI interactions from video. Automated framework for collecting high-fidelity UI action data from CAD software.
method_summary: Automated pipeline generating annotated CAD operation videos with keyframe extraction and action labeling. Enables training models for video-based CAD UI understanding and VQA tasks.

title: MiCADangelo: Fine-Grained Reconstruction of Constrained CAD Models from 3D Scans
abstract: Novel CAD reverse engineering method mimicking human design workflows using multi-plane 2D cross-sections to reconstruct detailed, editable, and fully parametric CAD models from 3D scans. First method incorporating sketch constraints directly into reconstruction.
method_summary: Analyzes 3D scans via 2D cross-sections to detect sketch planes, predicts constrained parametric sketches, and reconstructs fine-grained editable CAD models with geometric constraints.

title: CADCrafter: Generating Computer-Aided Design Models from Unconstrained Images
abstract: Image-to-parametric CAD model generation framework trained on synthetic data with geometry encoder and direct preference optimization to handle real-world unconstrained images. Bridges gap between synthetic training and real-world deployment.
method_summary: Geometry encoder extracts 3D-aware features from unconstrained images, combined with DPO alignment for robust CAD sequence generation. Trained on synthetic data, generalizes to real-world images.

title: CADDreamer: CAD Object Generation from Single-view Images
abstract: Generates CAD objects from single-view images using a primitive-aware multi-view diffusion model that perceives both local geometry and high-level structure for parametric CAD output.
method_summary: Primitive-aware multi-view diffusion model generates consistent multi-view representations from single images, then reconstructs parametric CAD models preserving geometric primitives.

title: Joint B-Rep Generation via Compositional k-Cell Particles
abstract: Encodes each topological entity as a composition of particles where adjacent cells share identical latents at interfaces, promoting geometric coupling along shared boundaries. Decouples rigid hierarchy for unified vertex/edge/face generation.
method_summary: Compositional k-cell particle representation where topology emerges from shared latent variables at cell interfaces. Enables joint generation of topology and geometry with global context awareness.
